_comment: Clinical decision pathway for Acute Sinusitis aligned to NHS Pharmacy First criteria and exclusion safeguards.
label: Sinusitis

questions.text: How long have you had sinus symptoms?
Do you have facial pain or pressure around your cheeks, forehead, or eyes?
Do you have a blocked or runny nose?
Do you have a severe headache?
Do you have reduced or lost sense of smell?
Do you have any visual changes, double vision, or eye swelling?
Do you have neurological symptoms (confusion, drowsiness, weakness, speech problems, neck stiffness, or light sensitivity)?
Have you had recurrent sinusitis episodes in the last year?
Do you have high fever (38°C or above) or feel systemically very unwell?

redFlags.description: Eye swelling or visual change suggests orbital complication
redFlags.message: Eye swelling or visual changes with sinus symptoms need emergency care now.

redFlags.description: Neurological symptoms suggest serious intracranial complication
redFlags.message: Neurological warning symptoms need emergency assessment. Call 999.

redFlags.description: Severe headache with systemic illness indicates urgent risk
redFlags.message: Severe headache with systemic illness needs urgent same-day clinical review.

eligibilityRules.reason: Symptoms under 10 days are usually viral and are outside Pharmacy First sinusitis treatment criteria
Chronic sinusitis (>12 weeks) requires GP assessment and possibly ENT referral

eligibilityRules.condition: q6 === true || q7 === true || q9 === true
eligibilityRules.reason: Red flag or severe systemic features require higher acuity care

eligibilityRules.condition: q1 === '10 days to 12 weeks' && q2 === true && q3 === true && q8 === 'No' && q6 === false && q7 === false && q9 === false
eligibilityRules.reason: Meets acute sinusitis eligibility criteria for pharmacy pathway

outcomeRules.reason: Likely viral phase under 10 days; self-care and monitoring advised
Acute sinusitis duration and symptom profile meet pharmacy treatment criteria
Outside sinusitis pharmacy criteria or exclusion factors present

selfCareAdvice: Steam inhalation, saline nasal spray, rest, paracetamol/ibuprofen. Most viral sinusitis resolves within 2–3 weeks.
pharmacyTreatmentOptions: Amoxicillin 500mg three times daily for 5 days
Doxycycline 200mg day 1 then 100mg daily for 4 days (if penicillin allergy)
safetyNetAdvice: Return if symptoms worsen rapidly, you develop eye swelling, severe headache, neck stiffness, or symptoms persist beyond 3 weeks.
pathwayPatientDisclaimer: This pathway supports disposition for acute sinus-type symptoms; features suggesting orbital involvement, severe headache, meningism, or sepsis need urgent in-person assessment.
